exam.name: GATE AE
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Aerospace Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Aerospace Engineering

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Vector and Matrix algebra, Systems of equations, Eigen values
Calculus: Single variable functions, Partial derivatives, Maxima/Minima, Vector calculus
Differential Equations: ODEs, higher order linear ODEs, and PDEs
Fourier Series and Laplace Transforms
Numerical Methods for algebraic equations, integration, and differentiation
Complex Analysis, Probability and Statistics
Atmosphere properties, Airspeeds, and Primary flight instruments
Airplane Performance: Drag polar, Take-off/Landing, Climb/Descent, Range/Endurance
Static Stability: Longitudinal, Directional and Lateral stability, Control derivatives
Dynamic Stability: Euler angles, Equations of motion, Decoupling, and Modes
Central force motion, Trajectory determination, Orbital period, and Kepler's laws
Basic Fluid Mechanics: Conservation laws, Dimensional analysis, and Viscous flows
Potential flow theory: Sources, Sinks, Doublets, and Line vortex
Airfoils and Wings: Thin airfoil theory, Finite wing theory, and Induced drag
Compressible Flows: Normal/Oblique shocks, Isentropic flow, Fanno/Rayleigh flows
Wind Tunnel Testing: Measurement and visualization techniques
Strength of Materials: Stress/Strain transformations, Mohr's circle, and Failure theories
Statically determinate and indeterminate trusses and beams, Column buckling
Flight vehicle structures: Thin-walled sections, Bending, Torsion, and Loads
Structural Dynamics: Free and forced vibrations of SDOF and 2-DOF systems
Theory of Elasticity: Equilibrium and Compatibility equations
Thermodynamics, Heat Transfer, and Combustion basics
Aerothermodynamics of Aircraft Engines: Brayton cycle and Engine performance
Turbomachinery: Axial and Centrifugal compressors, Axial turbines
Rockets: Thrust equation, Specific impulse, and Multi-staging
Non-rotating components, Turbine cooling, Surge and Stall

exam.name: GATE AG
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Agricultural Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Agricultural Engineering

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrices, Eigenvalues, and Linear Equations
Calculus: Limits, Continuity, Maxima/Minima, and Series
Vector Calculus: Differential Operators and Integration Theorems
Differential Equations: ODEs, PDEs, and Laplace Transforms
Probability and Statistics: Distributions and Regression
Numerical Methods: Integration and ODE Solutions
Machine Design: Selection of elements and Measurement of forces
Farm Machinery: Soil tillage, Hitch systems, and Equipment operation
Performance Parameters and Cost Analysis of Implements
Sources of Power: Renewable and Non-renewable farm power
Farm Power: I.C. Engine cycles, Systems, and Performance
Tractors and Power tillers: Transmission, Traction theory, and Testing
Fluid Mechanics: Properties, Bernoulli's theorem, and Pipe flow
Soil Mechanics: Properties, Permeability, and Shear strength
Hydrology: Cycle, Runoff estimation, and Hydrographs
Surveying and Leveling: Instruments and Computation of areas
Soil and Water Erosion: Mechanics, Control measures, and Dams
Watershed Management: Budgeting and Rainwater harvesting
Soil-Water-Plant Relationship: Water requirements and Infiltration
Irrigation Methods: Surface, Sprinkler, and Micro irrigation design
Agricultural Drainage: Surface and Sub-surface system design
Groundwater Hydrology: Aquifer flow and Recharge techniques
Wells and Pumps: Design, Construction, and Characteristics
Engineering properties of agriculture produce
Evaporation and Drying: Kinetics and Psychrometry
Size Reduction and Material Handling: Mechanics and Conveying
Processing of Seeds, Spices, Fruits, and Vegetables
Storage Systems: Controlled atmosphere and Silos
Heat and Mass Transfer: Conduction, Convection, and Radiation
Preservation of Food: Pasteurization, Sterilization, and Refrigeration

exam.name: GATE AR
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Architecture and Planning
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Architecture and Planning

topics.name: Architectural Graphics & Visual Composition in 2D and 3D
Computer Applications, Anthropometrics & Space Organization
Horizontal/Vertical Circulation, Space Standards & Universal Design
Building Byelaws, Codes and Standards
Project Management Techniques: PERT and CPM
Estimation, Specification, Professional Practice and Ethics
Form, Structure & Disaster Resistant Structures
Temporary Structures for Rehabilitation
Ecosystems, Ecological Principles & Environmental Considerations
Pollution Types, Controls and Abatement Strategies
Sustainable Development Goals, Climate Change & Built Environment
Climate Responsive Design
Urban Design: Historical Examples, Form, Pattern and Fabric
Concepts, Theories, Principles and Tools of Urban Design
Public Spaces, Spatial Qualities & Sense of Place
Urban Interventions, FAR, Densities & Building Byelaws
Heritage Conservation, Landscape Design & Site Planning
Urban Planning Theories: Eco-City and Smart City
Planners' Theories, Ekistics and Urban Sociology
Socio-Economic & Environmental Cost Benefit Analysis
Spatial Data Analysis & URDPFI Guidelines
Housing Typologies, Neighborhood Concepts & Examples
Residential Densities and Affordable Housing
Real Estate Valuation
Fire Fighting, Building Safety and Management Systems
Water Supply, Treatment, Distribution & Harvesting Systems
Sewage Disposal and Solid Waste Management (Recycling/Reuse)
Land-use-transportation Relationships & Road/Parking Design
Road Hierarchy, Level of Service & Pedestrian Planning
Principles of Art & Architecture; World History (Egyptian to Baroque)
Contemporary Trends: Art Nouveau to Deconstruction
Indian Vernacular, Traditional and Oriental Architecture
Works of Renowned National and International Architects

subjects.name: Building Construction and Structural Systems (Architecture B1)

topics.name: Construction Techniques, Prefabrication & Modular Coordination
Material Characteristics & Strength of Materials Principles
Structural Systems: Elastic/Limit State Design & Pre-stressing
High Rise & Long Span Structures: Gravity/Lateral Load Resisting
Solar Architecture & Thermal/Visual/Acoustic Comfort
Ventilation, Air-Conditioning & Sustainability Simulation
Plumbing, Electrification & Intelligent Buildings
Elevators and Escalators: Standards and Uses
Regional Delineation, Settlement Hierarchy & Plan Types
Government Schemes: TOD, SEZ, SRZ & Housing Policies
Slums, Squatters, Informal Housing & Special Needs Standards
GIS and Remote Sensing Applications in Planning
Physical, Land Use and Socio-economic Survey Tools
Urban Economics: Land Demand/Supply & Spatial Data Presentation
Governance, Legislation (PPP) & Infrastructure Management
Transportation Planning & Travel Demand Forecasting
Traffic Surveys, Flow Analysis & Management Controls
Mass Transportation & Intelligent Transportation Systems
Urban and Rural Infrastructure System Networks

exam.name: GATE BM
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Biomedical Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Biomedical Engineering

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrix algebra, systems of linear equations, eigenvalues and eigenvectors
Calculus: Multiple integrals, Vector identities, and Integral theorems
Differential Equations: ODEs, PDEs, and solution methods
Analysis of Complex Variables: Analytic functions and Residue theorem
Probability and Statistics: Distributions and Significance tests
Numerical Methods: Matrix inversion and iterative solutions
Circuit Elements and KCL/KVL laws
Transient analysis of RLC circuits and Network Theorems
AC quantities, Phasor analysis, and Series/Parallel resonance
Basic filters with R, L, C and Bode plots
Continuous/Discrete Signals, Sampling, and Fourier transforms
LTI Systems: Impulse response, Transfer function, and Convolution
Discrete time systems: Z-transform, DFT, and FIR/IIR filters
Semiconductor devices: Diode, BJT, and MOSFET
Op-Amp applications: Difference amplifier, instrumentation amp, and filters
Digital Logic: Combinational and Sequential circuits
Data Converters (ADC/DAC) and Microprocessor architecture
Measurement Errors and Expression of uncertainty
Measuring Instruments: PMMC, MI, Dynamometer, and Bridges
Basics of Control Systems and Transfer functions
Transducers: Resistive, capacitive, piezoelectric, and optical sensors
Biopotential measurement: ECG, EEG, EMG, ERG, and EOG
Clinical Measurements: Blood pressure, temperature, volume, and flow
Medical Equipment: Ventilator, Pacemaker, and Safety principles
Cell biology, Tissue types, and Homeostasis
Organ systems: Circulatory, Respiratory, and Nervous systems
X-Ray, Computed Tomography (CT), and Image formation
Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and Ultrasound
Nuclear Imaging: SPECT and PET
Kinematics of muscles/joints and Gait analysis
Hard and Soft Tissue mechanics: Bone, stress/strain, and viscoelasticity
Biofluid mechanics: Blood flow in the cardiovascular system
Properties of Biomaterials: Metallic, Ceramic, and Polymeric
Implants: Biocompatibility, Bioactivity, and Biodegradability
Drug delivery, Tissue engineering, and Characterization techniques

exam.name: GATE BT
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Biotechnology
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Biotechnology

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrices, Determinants, Systems of Equations, Eigen values/vectors
Calculus: Limits, Continuity, Partial derivatives, Maxima/Minima, Sequences and series
Differential Equations: Linear/Nonlinear first order ODEs, Higher order ODEs, Laplace transforms
Probability and Statistics: Distributions, Correlation and regression analysis
Numerical Methods: Algebraic equations, Integration (Trapezoidal/Simpson), Single step ODE methods
Biochemistry: Biomolecules, Metabolism regulation, Photosynthesis, Respiration, Enzyme kinetics/inhibition
Microbiology: Bacterial/Viral classification, Microbial ecology, Growth kinetics, Antibiotics/Resistance
Immunology: Innate/Adaptive immunity, Antibody structure, MHC, Vaccines, Hypersensitivity/Autoimmunity
Genetics and Evolutionary Biology: Mendelian inheritance, Recombination, Population genetics, Speciation
Cell Biology: Cell structure, Cycle control, Signaling/Transduction, Protein trafficking, Autophagy
Molecular Biology: Gene/Chromosome structure, Replication, Transcription, Translation, DNA repair
Engineering Principles: Material/Energy balances, Stoichiometry of growth, Electron balance
Classical Thermodynamics and Bioenergetics: Laws, Phase/Reaction equilibria, Metabolic energetics
Transport Processes: Newtonian/Non-Newtonian fluids, Mixing, Oxygen transfer (kLa), Heat exchangers
Bioreaction Engineering: Rate laws, Ideal reactors (Batch/Mixed/Plug), Enzyme immobilization, Scale-up
Upstream and Downstream Processing: Sterilization, Filtration, Centrifugation, Chromatography, Extraction
Instrumentation and Process Control: Measurement devices, First/Second order systems, PID controllers
Plants: Totipotency, Tissue culture, Secondary metabolites, Transgenic plants, Gene transfer
Animals: Cell culture media, Hybridoma technology, Stem cells, Cloning, Transgenic animals
Microbes: Biomass production, Biofuels, Bioplastics, Industrial enzymes, Recombinant protein purification

subjects.name: Recombinant DNA technology and Other Tools

topics.name: Recombinant DNA technology: Restriction enzymes, Vectors, cDNA/Genomic libraries, Gene targeting
Molecular tools: PCR, DNA/RNA labeling, Blotting, DNA fingerprinting, CRISPR-Cas, Biosensors
Analytical tools: Microscopy, Spectroscopy (UV/NMR/MS), Electrophoresis, Micro-arrays, Flow cytometry
Computational tools: Bioinformatics databases, Sequence alignment, Phylogeny, Systems biology

exam.name: GATE CE
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Civil Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Civil Engineering

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrix algebra, Systems of linear equations, Eigenvalues and eigenvectors
Calculus: Single variable functions, Mean value theorems, Maxima/Minima, Vectors, Line/Surface/Volume integrals
Ordinary Differential Equation (ODE): First/Higher order linear equations, Euler-Cauchy, Initial/Boundary value problems
Partial Differential Equation (PDE): Fourier series, Separation of variables, Diffusion/Wave/Laplace equations
Probability and Statistics: Sampling, Conditional probability, Descriptive statistics, Random variables, Regression
Numerical Methods: Error analysis, Algebraic equations, Polynomials, Numerical integration and ODE solutions
Engineering Mechanics: Force systems, Equilibrium, Internal forces, Friction, Center of mass, Free vibrations (SDOF)
Solid Mechanics: Bending moment/Shear force, Stress/Strain, Torsion, Buckling of columns
Structural Analysis: Determinate/Indeterminate structures, Trusses, Arches, Cables, Displacement methods, Stiffness/Flexibility
Construction Materials and Management: Steel/Concrete properties, Project planning (PERT/CPM), Cost estimation
Concrete Structures: Working stress and Limit state design (Beams, Slabs, Columns), Prestressed concrete
Steel Structures: Design of Tension/Compression members, Connections (Simple/Eccentric), Plate girders, Plastic analysis
Soil Mechanics: Phase relationships, Classification, Permeability, Seepage, Effective stress, Consolidation, Shear strength
Foundation Engineering: Sub-surface investigations, Earth pressure theories, Slope stability, Bearing capacity, Deep foundations
Fluid Mechanics: Fluid statics, Conservation equations, Laminar/Turbulent flow, Pipe networks, Boundary layer, Lift/Drag
Hydraulics: Specific energy, Hydraulic jump, Uniform and Gradually varied flow, Hydraulic similitude
Hydrology: Hydrologic cycle, Unit hydrographs, Flood routing, Groundwater hydrology, Darcy's Law
Irrigation: Crop water requirements (Duty/Delta), Gravity dams, Spillways, Canal design, Cross drainage
Water and Waste Water Quality and Treatment: Standards, Unit processes, Distribution, Sewerage design, Sludge disposal
Air Pollution: Pollutant types, Impacts, Air quality standards and control
Municipal Solid Wastes: Generation, Collection, Transportation, and Engineered management systems
Transportation Infrastructure: Geometric design of highways, Railway track (Speed/Cant), Airport runway/taxiway design
Highway Pavements: Materials, Bituminous mixes, Design of flexible and rigid pavements using IRC codes
Traffic Engineering: Traffic studies, flow parameters, Signal design (Webster's), Intersections, Highway capacity
Principles of Surveying: Errors, Leveling, Traversing, Triangulation, Total station, Horizontal/Vertical curves
Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing: Scale, Flying height, Basics of Remote Sensing and GIS

exam.name: GATE CH
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Chemical Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Chemical Engineering

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrix algebra, Systems of equations, Eigenvalues and eigenvectors
Calculus: Single variable functions, Vector calculus, Line/Surface/Volume integrals, and Theorems (Stokes, Gauss, Green)
Differential Equations: First/Higher order ODEs, Cauchy/Euler equations, Laplace transforms, and solutions to Heat/Wave/Laplace equations
Complex Variables: Polar form, triangle inequality, and Complex numbers
Probability and Statistics: Distributions (Poisson, Normal, Binomial), Mean/Standard deviation, and Regression analysis
Numerical Methods: Solutions of algebraic equations, Integration (Trapezoidal/Simpson), and ODE solution methods
Process Calculations: Mass and energy balances (steady/unsteady), Recycle, bypass, and purge, and Degree of freedom analysis
Thermodynamics: First/Second laws, Entropy, and Pure substance properties
Mixtures and Equilibria: Partial molar properties, activity coefficients, VLE prediction, and Chemical reaction equilibrium
Fluid Mechanics: Bernoulli equation, Newtonian/non-Newtonian fluids, Flow meters, Boundary layer, and Turbulent flow
Mechanical Operations: Size reduction/classification, settling, Centrifuge/Cyclones, and Agitation/Mixing
Heat Transfer Fundamentals: Conduction, Convection, Radiation, and Heat transfer coefficients
Phase Change and Evaporation: Boiling, condensation, and Single/Multiple effect evaporators
Heat Exchanger Design: Double pipe and Shell and tube heat exchangers
Diffusion and Theories: Fick's laws, molecular diffusion, and Mass transfer theories (film, penetration, surface renewal)
Transfer Operations: HTU/NTU concepts, stage efficiencies, and momentum/heat/mass analogies
Equipment Design: Distillation, Absorption, Leaching, Extraction, Drying, and Membrane separations
Reactor Kinetics: Theories of reaction rates, homogeneous reactions, and ideal/non-ideal reactors
Catalysis and Non-isothermal Systems: Michaelis-Menten kinetics, Heterogeneous catalysis, and catalyst deactivation
Reactor RTD: Residence time distribution and single parameter models
Process Dynamics: Measurement variables, transfer functions, and dynamic responses
Control Systems: P/PI/PID modes, control valves, and closed-loop stability analysis
Advanced Control: Cascade, feed forward control, and frequency response
Process Economics: Cost estimation, depreciation, and profitability (ROI, Payback, Discounted cash flow)
Process Optimization and Equipment Sizing
Inorganic and Fertilizers: Sulfuric/Phosphoric acids, Chlor-alkali, Ammonia, and Urea
Natural Products and Refining: Pulp/Paper, Sugar, Oils/Fats, and Petroleum refining
Polymerization Industries: Polyethylene, PVC, and Synthetic fibers

exam.name: GATE CY
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Chemistry
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Chemistry

topics.name: Quantum Mechanics: Postulates, Operators, Schrödinger equations, Particle in a box, Harmonic oscillator, Rigid rotor, and Hydrogen-like atoms
Approximation Methods and Molecular Bonding: Variation method, Perturbation, LCAO-MO theory, MOT, and Hückel approximation
Group Theory: Symmetry elements, Point groups, Character tables, and internal coordinates
Spectroscopy: Atomic, Rotational, Vibrational, electronic, Raman, and Basic principles of NMR
Equilibrium: Laws of thermodynamics, partial molar quantities, Chemical equilibria, and Phase rule
Electrochemistry: Ionic mobility, Debye-Hückel limiting law, Nernst Equation, and Electrochemical cells
Statistical Thermodynamics, Kinetics and Surfaces: Ensembles, partition functions, TST, Eyring equation, Catalysis, and Adsorption isotherms
Main Group Elements: Boranes, carboranes, silicones, silicates, phosphazenes, and Noble gas chemistry
Transition Elements: Coordination structure, CFT, MOT, Orgel/Tanabe-Sugano diagrams, and Magnetic properties
Lanthanides, Actinides, and Radioactivity: Recovery, periodic properties, decay processes, fission, and fusion
Organometallics: 18-Electron rule, fluxionality, and homogeneous/heterogeneous catalysis (Hydrogenation, Ziegler-Natta)
Bioinorganic Chemistry and Solids: Ion transport, metalloenzymes, crystal systems, and Band theory
Instrumental Methods of Analysis: UV-visible, FTIR, NMR, ESR, MS, Mossbauer spectroscopy, and Chromatography
Stereochemistry: Chirality, absolute configurations, stereoselective synthesis, and conformational analysis
Reaction Mechanisms and Intermediates: Hammond's postulate, substitution/addition/elimination reactions, and reactive species (carbocations, carbenes, etc.)
Organic Synthesis: Coupling reactions (Heck, Suzuki), metal-based reagents, and retrosynthetic analysis
Asymmetric Synthesis and Selectivity: Resolution, organocatalysis, and stereoselective addition to C=O groups
Pericyclic Reactions and Photochemistry: Electrocyclic/Sigmatropic reactions, Woodward-Hoffmann rule, and Norrish cleavage
Heterocyclic Compounds, Biomolecules, and Techniques: Pyrrole, Pyridine, Sugars, Proteins, and structural determination using Spectroscopy

exam.name: GATE DA
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Data Science and Artificial Intelligence
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Data Science and Artificial Intelligence organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Counting, probability axioms, Bayes Theorem, and conditional probability
Discrete/Continuous random variables and distributions (Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Normal, t, chi-squared)
Central limit theorem, confidence intervals, and hypothesis testing (z-test, t-test, chi-squared test)
Vector spaces, subspaces, linear independence, and systems of linear equations
Matrices, Gaussian elimination, eigenvalues, eigenvectors, and LU decomposition
Singular Value Decomposition (SVD), determinants, rank, and projections
Single variable functions, limits, continuity, and differentiability
Taylor series, maxima, minima, and single variable optimization
Programming in Python and basic data structures (stacks, queues, linked lists, trees, hash tables)
Search (linear/binary) and basic sorting algorithms (selection, bubble, insertion, merge, quicksort)
Graph theory basics, traversals, and shortest path algorithms
ER-model, Relational model, SQL, integrity constraints, and normal forms
Data transformation (normalization, sampling, compression) and file organization
Data warehouse modelling: schemas for multidimensional data and concept hierarchies
Supervised Learning: Linear/Logistic regression, KNN, Naive Bayes, SVM, and Decision trees
Neural Networks: Multi-layer perceptron and feed-forward networks
Unsupervised Learning: k-means/k-medoid clustering and hierarchical clustering
Dimensionality reduction: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and evaluation methods (cross-validation)
Search strategies: Informed, uninformed, and adversarial search
Logic: Propositional and predicate logic
Reasoning under uncertainty: Variable elimination and approximate inference

exam.name: GATE EC
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Electronics and Communication Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Electronics and Communication Engineering organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Vector space, Basis, Matrix algebra, Eigen values/vectors, and Rank
Calculus: Definite/improper integrals, Partial derivatives, Maxima/minima, and Multiple integrals
Differential Equations: Higher order linear ODEs, Cauchy/Euler equations, and PDEs
Vector Analysis: Gradient, Divergence, Curl, and Vector theorems (Gauss, Green, Stokes)
Complex Analysis: Analytic functions, Cauchy's integral formula, and Residue theorem
Probability and Statistics: Distributions (Normal, Poisson, Exponential) and Joint/Conditional probability
Circuit Analysis: Node/Mesh analysis, Network theorems, and Sinusoidal steady state
Time and Frequency domain analysis: RLC circuits and Laplace transform solutions
Continuous-time Signals: Fourier series, Fourier transform, and Sampling theorem
Discrete-time Signals: DTFT, DFT, and Z-transform
LTI Systems: Causality, Stability, Convolution, and Frequency response
Semiconductor Physics: Energy bands, Carrier concentration, and Transport (Drift/Diffusion)
P-N Junction and Diodes: Zener, LED, Photo diode, and Solar cell
Transistor Physics: BJT, MOS capacitor, and MOSFET characteristics
Diode Circuits: Clipping, Clamping, and Rectifiers
BJT and MOSFET Amplifiers: Biasing, Small signal analysis, and Frequency response
Op-amp Circuits: Amplifiers, Filters, Schmitt triggers, and Oscillators
Combinatorial Circuits: Boolean algebra, K-Maps, CMOS implementations, and MUX/Decoders
Sequential Circuits: Latches, Flip-flops, Counters, and Finite State Machines
Data Converters and Memories: ADC, DAC, ROM, SRAM, and DRAM
Computer Organization: Machine instructions, ALU, and Instruction pipelining
System Modeling: Transfer functions, Block diagrams, and Signal flow graphs
Time/Frequency Analysis: Transient response, Routh-Hurwitz, and Stability criteria
Stability and Design: Bode plots, Root-locus, Nyquist, and Compensation
State Variable Model: State equations and LTI system solutions
Random Processes: Power spectral density and Filtering of white noise
Analog Communications: AM, FM, Spectra, and Super heterodyne receivers
Digital Communications: PCM, DPCM, ASK, PSK, FSK, QAM, and Matched filter receivers
Information Theory and Error Correction: Entropy, Channel capacity, and Hamming codes
Maxwell's Equations and Plane Waves: Propagation, Polarization, and Poynting vector
Transmission Lines: Characteristic impedance, Impedance matching, and Smith chart
Waveguides and Antennas: Light propagation in fibers, Dipole/Monopole antennas, and Arrays

exam.name: GATE ES
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Environmental Science and Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Environmental Science and Engineering organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Determinants, matrices, systems of equations, and Eigenvalues
Calculus: Limits, continuity, derivatives, Taylor series, integrals, and partial derivatives
Differential Equations: First order ODEs, higher order linear ODEs, and Laplace transforms
Probability and Statistics: Descriptive statistics, distributions, Bayes theorem, and Hypothesis testing
Fundamentals of Environmental Chemistry: Chemical bonding, kinetics, and thermodynamics
Principles of Water Chemistry: Quality parameters, Acid-base equilibria, and carbonate system
Soil Chemistry: Organic matter, nitrogen/phosphorous cycles, and cation exchange capacity
Atmospheric Chemistry: Composition, ozone formation, and urban smog
Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic Microorganisms: Diversity, classification, and interactions
Cell Chemistry and Biology: Biomolecules structure and organelle functions
Microbial Metabolism: Glycolysis, TCA cycle, ETC, and enzyme kinetics
Growth and Control of Microorganisms: Nutrition, growth models, and physical/chemical control
Microbiology and Health: Pathogens, transmission, and coliform quantification
Global and Surface Water Resources: Hydrological cycle, runoff, and reservoir routing
Groundwater Resources: Aquifer parameters, Darcy's law, and well hydraulics
Environmental Hydraulics: Fluid properties, pipe networks, and channel hydraulics
Water and Wastewater Quality: Quality parameters and River pollution models
Water Treatment Methods: Screening, sedimentation, filtration, and disinfection
Wastewater Treatment: Sewage treatment (primary to tertiary) and sludge disposal
Industrial Effluents and Zero Liquid Discharge
Reactor Kinetics and Design: Mass balance and reactor types (Batch, CMF, Plug flow)
Atmospheric Pollution: Sources, transport, and air quality standards
Particulate Pollutants: Control methods (Cyclones, ESP, Bag-house filters)
Gaseous Pollutants: Control by absorption, adsorption, and combustion
Air Quality Management: Meteorology and dispersion modelling
Noise Pollution: Measurement, standards, and control
Municipal Solid Waste Management: Generation, collection, and disposal processes
Hazardous Waste Management: Fate, treatment, and soil contamination
Special Wastes: Management of biomedical, plastic, and E-waste
Global Air Pollution Effects: Warming, climate change, and ozone hole
Ecology and Biodiversity: Ecosystems and environmental degradation factors
Environmental Management and Auditing: EMS, ISO14000, and EIA
Environmental Law and Policy: National Acts and International treaties
Energy and Environment: Renewable sources and the energy-environment nexus
Sustainable Development: Goals, hurdles, and economics

exam.name: GATE EY
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Ecology and Evolution
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Ecology and Evolution organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Fundamental Concepts: Abiotic/Biotic components, scales, niches and habitats
Population Ecology: Growth rates, metapopulation, and age-structured populations
Interactions: Symbiosis, competition, predation, and Lotka-Voltera equations
Community Ecology: Assembly, succession, diversity indices, and island biogeography
Ecosystems Structure and Function: Trophic levels, nutrient cycles, and productivity
History of Evolutionary Thought: Lamarckism, Darwinism, and Modern Synthesis
Fundamentals: Variation, natural selection, fitness, and types of selection
Diversity of Life: Origin of life and systems of classification
Life History Strategies: Resource allocation, tradeoffs, and r/K selection
Evolutionary Interactions: Co-evolution, arms race, and mimicry
Population and Quantitative Genetics: Hardy-Weinberg, drift, and phenotypic plasticity
Molecular Evolution and Phylogenetics: Neutral theory, molecular clocks, and reconstruction
Macroevolution: Speciation, adaptive radiation, and convergence
Mathematics and Statistics in Ecology: Functions, derivatives, probability, and distributions
Statistical Hypothesis Testing: p-values, t-test, Chi-square, regression, and ANOVA
Classical Ethology: Instinct, imprinting, and learnt behavior
Sensory Ecology: Neuroethology, communication signaling, and recognition systems
Foraging Ecology: Foraging behaviour and optimal foraging theory
Reproduction: Sexual selection, mating systems, and parental care
Social Living: Group-living costs/benefits, kin selection, and altruism
Biodiversity and Conservation: Threats, hotspots, and conservation genetics
Disease Ecology and Evolution: Epidemiology, zoonotic diseases, and antibiotic resistance
Plant and Animal Breeding: Marker assisted breeding and trait genetics
Global Climate Change: Causes, consequences, and mitigation

exam.name: GATE GE
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Geomatics Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Geomatics Engineering organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Surveying measurements, Accuracy, and Precision
Errors and their adjustments
Regression analysis and Correlation coefficient
Least square adjustment
Statistical significance and Chi square test
Basic concept and Electromagnetic spectrum
Spectral signature and Resolutions (Spectral, Spatial, Temporal, Radiometric)
Platforms, Sensors, and Data Products (PAN, Microwave, Thermal, Hyperspectral)
Visual and digital interpretation methods
GNSS Principle and Components
GNSS Data collection methods and DGPS
Errors in observations and corrections
GIS Data Sources, Models, and Structures
DBMS and Creation of spatial and non-spatial Databases
Spatial analysis: Interpolation, Buffer, and Overlay
Terrain Modeling and Network analysis
Maps: Types, Scales, Numbering, and Coordinate systems
Map projections and Datums (Geoid, Spheroid, WGS-84)
Land Surveying: Levelling methods and Compass/Theodolite/Total Station
Trigonometric levelling and Tachometer
Traversing, Triangulation, and Trilateration
Types of photographs, Flying height, and Scale
Relief displacement, Stereoscopy, and 3-D Models
Height determination using Parallax Bar, DEM, and Slope
Sampling, Quantization, and Fourier Transform
Digital image characteristics: Histogram and Scattergram
Radiometric and Geometric Corrections: Registration and Resampling
Image Enhancement: Contrast and Spatial filters
Image Transformation: PCA, Color transformations, and Indices (NDVI/NDWI)
Image Segmentation and Classification techniques

exam.name: GATE ST
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Statistics
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Statistics organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Sets, Real number system, and Sequences
Series of real numbers and Power series
Functions of a real variable: Limits, Continuity, Differentiability, and Riemann integration
Functions of several real variables: Partial derivatives, Taylor's theorem, and Multiple integrals
Vector Spaces, Basis, Dimension, Rank-Nullity, and Systems of equations
Inner products, Gram-Schmidt, Eigen values/vectors, and SVD
Axiomatic probability, Bayes' theorem, and Independence
Random variables, Expectation, Moments, and Inequalities
Standard Discrete Distributions: Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Geometric
Standard Continuous Distributions: Normal, Gamma, Beta, Weibull
Jointly distributed random variables, Marginal/Conditional distributions, and Order statistics
Multinomial, Bivariate normal, and Sampling distributions
Convergence modes, Law of Large Numbers, and Central Limit Theorem
Markov chains: Classification of states and Limiting behavior
Poisson, Birth-and-Death processes, and Brownian motion
Sufficiency, Completeness, UMVUE, and Rao-Blackwell theorem
Methods of Estimation: MLE and Method of Moments
Interval estimation and Confidence intervals
Neyman-Pearson lemma and UMP tests
Likelihood ratio tests and Large sample tests
Goodness of fit, Chi-square, KS test, and Rank-based tests
Multivariate Normal distribution, Hotelling's T2, and Wishart distribution
Simple/Multiple linear regression and Gauss-Markov theorem

exam.name: GATE GG
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Geology and Geophysics
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Geology and Geophysics organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Earth and planetary system: internal structure, composition, isostasy, and plate tectonics
Elements of seismology, geomagnetism, and paleomagnetism
Weathering, soil formation, and landforms (river, wind, glacier, ocean, volcanoes)
Structural geology, Crystallography, and Mineralogy of rock forming minerals
Petrology, Geological time scale, and Stratigraphic principles of India
Mineral, coal and petroleum resources of India
Applied Geosciences: Remote sensing, hydrogeology, and prospecting methods
Geomorphology and Structural Geology: Rock deformation, geometry of structures, and map interpretation
Crystallography, Mineralogy, and Geochemistry: Cosmic abundance, cycles, and thermodynamics
Petrology: Classification and genesis of Igneous, Sedimentary, and Metamorphic rocks
Paleobiology and Stratigraphy: Diversity of life, fossilization, and Indian stratigraphy
Resource Geology: Ore-mineralogy, coal/petroleum geology, and exploration techniques
Applied Geology: Rock mechanics, natural hazards, hydrogeology, and GIS
Solid-Earth Geophysics and Geodesy: Gravity/magnetic fields, Earth interior, and GPS
Earthquake Seismology and Potential Fields: Elasticity theory, wave propagation, and field equations
Gravity and Magnetic Methods: Corrections, data reduction, and anomaly interpretation
Electrical and Electromagnetic Methods: Resistivity, IP, EM induction, and GPR
Seismic Methods and Signal Processing: Data acquisition, processing (CDP), and transforms
Geophysical Well Logging and Radioactive Methods
Geophysical Inversion: Forward/inverse problems, regularization, and machine learning

exam.name: GATE NM
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Naval Architecture and Marine Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Naval Architecture and Marine Engineering organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Determinants, matrices, and systems of linear equations
Calculus and Vector Analysis: Gradient, divergence, curl, and integral theorems (Stokes, Gauss, Green)
Differential Equations: Ordinary and partial differential equations, and separation of variables
Complex Analysis, Fourier Series, and Laplace Transformations
Numerical Methods, Probability, and Statistics
Engineering Mechanics: Free-body diagrams, trusses, and kinematics/dynamics of rigid bodies
Mechanics of Materials: Stress/strain, Mohr's circle, and failure theories
Structural Analysis: Shear force, bending moment diagrams, torsion, and Euler's columns
Vibrations: Free and forced vibrations of damped and undamped systems
Machine Design: Static/dynamic loading, gears, bearings, and joining techniques (welding, bolting)
Fluid Properties and Statics: Stability of floating bodies and conservation laws
Potential Flow: Sources, sinks, doublets, and Kutta-Joukowski theorem
Vortex Motion: Biot-Savart's law, vortex filaments, and vortex sheets
Viscous Flow: Navier-Stokes equations, Couette flow, and Poiseuille flow
Boundary Layer Theory: Prandtl's equations, Blasius solution, and separation criteria
Airfoils and Wings: Lift, drag, thin aerofoil theory, and cavitation
Marine Hydrodynamics: Added-mass, slender-body theory, and wave/body forces
Ship Geometry and Hydrostatics: Archimedes' principle, buoyancy, and laws of flotation
Stability and Trim: Statical/dynamical stability, inclining experiment, and damage stability
Resistance and Propulsion: Model testing, screw propeller geometry, and hull-propeller interaction
Ship Manoeuvring and Motions: Equations of motion, rudder hydrodynamics, and ship motion stabilisers
Ocean Waves: Regular/irregular waves, wave spectrum, and seakeeping
Ship Structures and Strength: Framing systems, hull girder strength, and fatigue/fracture
Physical Oceanography, Offshore Structures, and Port Engineering
Thermodynamics: First and second laws, Carnot cycle, and entropy
Gas Power and Air Standard Cycles: Otto, Diesel, Dual, and Brayton cycles
Internal Combustion Engines: Spark and Compression ignition, and combustion stages
Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Principles
Marine Diesel Engines: Scavenging, turbocharging, and EEDI regulations
Steam and Gas Turbines: Construction, compounding, and combined cycles
Propulsion Systems: Nuclear, electrical, and automation of propulsion plants
Marine Boilers and Engine Dynamics: Torsional vibrations and critical speeds
Marine Auxiliary Machinery: Pumps, piping systems, and cargo handling

exam.name: GATE PI
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Production and Industrial Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Production and Industrial Engineering organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrix algebra, Systems of equations, Eigen values/vectors
Calculus: Single variable functions, Vector calculus, and Integral theorems
Differential Equations: First/Higher order ODEs, Cauchy/Euler equations, and Laplace transforms
Complex Variables: Analytic functions, Cauchy's integral theorem, and Taylor series
Probability and Statistics: Sampling theorems, Distributions, and Linear regression
Numerical Methods: Linear/Nonlinear algebraic equations and Integration rules
Engineering Materials: Properties, Applications, and Iron-carbon phase diagram
Applied Mechanics: Equivalent force systems, Trusses, and Free body concepts
Strength of Materials: Stress/strain relations, Mohr's circle, Failure theories, and Beam deflection
Theory of Machines and Design: Planar mechanisms, Cams, Governors, and Joint design
Thermal and Fluids Engineering: Fluid statics, Bernoulli's equation, Laws of thermodynamics, and Heat transfer
Casting: Sand casting, Gating systems, Riser design, and Solidification analysis
Metal Forming: Elastic/Plastic deformation, Yield criteria, Bulk forming, and Sheet metal working
Joining of Materials: Fusion welding, Solid state welding, and Adhesive joining
Powder Processing: Production, Compaction, and Sintering
Polymers and Composites: Injection molding, Blow molding, and Composite fabrication
Machining: Orthogonal/Oblique machining, Cutting forces, Tool life, and Merchant's analysis
Finishing Processes: Grinding, Honing, Lapping, and Super-finishing
Machine Tools: Construction and Kinematics of Lathe, Milling, and Drilling machines
Advanced Manufacturing: Non-traditional machining (EDM, ECM, USM, LBM) and Additive manufacturing
Computer Integrated Manufacturing: CAD/CAM, CNC, Industrial Robots, and FMS
Metrology and Inspection: Limits, Fits, Tolerances, and Geometric measurements
Quality Management: SQC, Control charts, Six Sigma, and ISO standards
Reliability and Maintenance: MTBF, MTTR, System reliability, and Preventive maintenance
Product Design and Development: Life cycle, Standardization, and Value engineering
Work System Design: Productivity, Method study, Work measurement, and Ergonomics
Facility Design: Plant layout, Assembly line balancing, and Materials handling
Operation Research: Linear programming, Simplex, Transportation models, and Queuing
Engineering Economy and Costing: Depreciation, Break-even analysis, and Capital investment
Production Control: Forecasting, APP, MRP, ERP, JIT, and Supply Chain
Project Management: Scheduling with CPM, PERT, and GERT

exam.name: GATE TF
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Textile Engineering and Fibre Science
exam.description: National level examination for admission to postgraduate programs in Textile Engineering and Fibre Science

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrices, Determinants, Systems of Equations, Eigen values and vectors
Calculus: Limits, Continuity, Differentiability, Partial differentiation, Integrals, and Series
Differential Equations: First/Higher order linear ODEs, Euler-Cauchy, and PDEs (Wave, Heat, Laplace)
Probability and Statistics: Distributions (Poisson, Binomial, Normal), ANOVA, and Regression
Numerical Methods: Algebraic equations, Trapezoidal/Simpson's rules, and ODE methods
Natural Fibres: Structure, properties and uses of cotton, wool, silk, and bast fibres
Polymer Science: Molecular architecture, phases, Glass transition (Tg), and Melting (Tm)
Man-made Fibre Spinning: Melt, Wet, Dry, Dry-jet-wet, and Gel spinning processes
Post Spinning and Texturing: Drawing, heat setting, and different texturing methods
Investigative Methods: XRD, SEM/TEM, IR Spectroscopy, DSC, and TGA
Opening and Cleaning: Ginning, blow room principles, and modern carding machines
Drawing and Combing: Roller drawing, auto levellers, and combing cycle mechanisms
Roving and Ring Spinning: Drafting, twisting, and bobbin/cop building
Advanced Spinning Systems: Rotor, air-jet, friction, compact, and twist-less spinning
Yarn Structure and Physics: Packing density, twist-strength relations, and fibre migration
Fabric Preparation: Winding, warping creels, sizing systems, and drawing-in
Weaving Mechanics: Shedding (Tappet, Dobby, Jacquard), Weft insertion, and Sley kinematics
Shuttle-less Weaving: Projectile, rapier, water-jet, air-jet, and multiphase looms
Knitting and Nonwovens: Weft/Warp knitting constructions, web formation, and bonding
Fabric Geometry: Peirce's equations, Munden's constants, and tightness factors
Sampling and Moisture: Sampling techniques, errors, and moisture in textiles
Fibre and Yarn Testing: Length, HVI, Twist, hairiness, and evenness testing
Fabric Testing: Stiffness, drape, tensile strength, and air permeability
Preparatory Processes: Singeing, mercerization, and preparatory cycles for cotton/wool/silk
Dyeing Principles: Classification of dyes, Dyeing machines, and thermodynamics of dyeing
Colour Measurement: Beer-Lambert's law, Kubelka-Munk theory, and fastness determination
Printing Styles: Roller, Screen, Inkjet, and Resist/Discharge styles
Finishing and Pollution Control: Mechanical finishing, functional finishes, and effluent treatment

exam.name: GATE MT
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Metallurgical Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Metallurgical Engineering organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrices, Determinants, Systems of linear equations, Eigen values and Eigen vectors
Calculus and Vector Calculus: Limits, Derivatives, Maxima/Minima, Vector theorems (Stokes, Gauss, Green)
Differential Equations: ODEs (Linear/Non-linear), higher order linear ODEs, and PDEs (Heat/Wave)
Probability and Statistics: Sampling theorems, Distributions, and Linear least squares
Numerical Methods: Root finding (Newton-Raphson), Integration (Simpson), and ODE methods
Laws of Thermodynamics: Energy conservation, Entropy, Free energy, and Maxwell's relations
Chemical Potential and Solutions: Ideal/Regular solutions and Applications to metallurgical systems
Phase Equilibria: Gibbs phase rule, Binary phase diagrams, Lever rule, and Ellingham diagrams
Electrochemistry: Nernst equation and Potential-pH diagrams
Momentum Transfer: Viscosity, Bernoulli's equation, and Flow through pipes
Heat and Mass Transfer: Conduction, Convection, Radiation, and Fick's laws of diffusion
Chemical and Electrochemical Kinetics: Reaction rates, Arrhenius relation, and Polarization
Mineral Beneficiation: Comminution, Classification, Flotation, and Agglomeration
Extractive Metallurgy: Material/Energy balances and Non-ferrous extraction (Al, Cu, Ti)
Iron and Steel Making: Blast furnace balance, Slag properties, and Primary steel making (BOF, EAF)
Secondary Steel Making and Continuous Casting: Ladle processes, Degassing, and casting dynamics
Crystal Structure and XRD: Chemical bonding, Bragg's law, and SEM imaging
Imperfections and Diffusion in Solids: Point/Line/Surface defects and Diffusion equations
Phase Transformations: Nucleation, Solidification, and Solid-state transformations (Precipitation, Martensite)
Heat Treatment and Properties: TTT/CCT diagrams, Surface hardening, and Material properties
Elasticity and Plasticity: Stress/Strain tensors, Mohr's circle, Yield criteria, and Slip/Twinning
Dislocation Theory: Types of dislocations, Stress fields, and Interactions
Strengthening Mechanisms: Work hardening, Solid solution, and Precipitation strengthening
Fracture, Fatigue, and Creep: Griffith theory, Cyclic behavior, and High temperature deformation
Metal Casting: Mould design, Feeding/Gating systems, and Casting defects
Metal Forming: Rolling, Forging, Extrusion, and Sheet metal forming
Metal Joining and Powder Metallurgy: Welding, Brazing, Soldering, and Powder processing
Non-destructive Testing (NDT): Ultrasonic, Radiography, and Magnetic particle inspection

exam.name: GATE IN
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Instrumentation Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Instrumentation Engineering organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrix algebra, systems of equations, consistency, rank, and Eigenvalues
Calculus: Mean value theorems, partial derivatives, multiple integrals, and vector theorems
Differential Equations: First/second order linear ODEs, Cauchy/Euler, and PDE solutions
Analysis of Complex Variables: Analytic functions, Cauchy’s formulas, and Residue theorem
Probability and Statistics: Sampling, distributions (Normal, Poisson, Binomial), and random variables
Numerical Methods: Matrix inversion, iterative solutions for ODEs, and regression analysis
Electrostatics: Coulomb's Law, Electric Field Intensity, Gauss's Law, and Capacitance
Magnetostatics: Biot Savart's law, Ampere's law, Faraday's law, and Inductance
Circuit Analysis: Voltage/Current sources, Kirchhoff's laws, and Network theorems
Transient and AC Analysis: RLC transients, Phasor analysis, and Resonance
Network Parameters: One-port/Two-port networks and driving point impedance
Electrical Machines: Transformers and Three phase induction motors principles
Signal Transforms: Laplace, Fourier, and z-transforms of periodic/aperiodic signals
LTI Systems: Frequency response, Convolution, correlation, and FIR/IIR filters
Feedback and Stability: Bode plot, Routh/Nyquist criteria, and Compensator design
Control Components: Servo/Stepper motors, Synchro pairs, and Control valves
Controllers: P, PI, PID, Cascade, Feed-forward controllers, and PID tuning
Transistor Circuits: Characteristics of Diodes, BJTs, and MOSFETs; Small signal analysis
Op-amp Applications: Instrumentation amplifiers, active filters, Oscillators, and PLL
Digital Logic: Combinational/Sequential circuits, Schmitt triggers, and Timers
Data Converters: ADC (Integrating, Successive approximation, Flash, Sigma-delta) and DAC
Embedded Systems and PLCs: Microprocessors, DCS, and Programmable Logic Controllers
Measurement Fundamentals: SI units, standards, error analysis, and uncertainty
Bridges: Wheatstone, Kelvin, Maxwell, and Wien for R, L, C measurement
Electronic Instrumentation: DMM, Digital voltmeter, and Oscilloscope
Sensors: Resistive, capacitive, inductive, piezoelectric, and Hall effect sensors
Industrial Transducers: Pressure, Flow (Variable head, Ultrasonic, Turbine), and Temperature (RTD, Thermocouple)
Analytical Instruments: pH, conductivity, and viscosity measurement
Communication: AM, FM, Pulse code modulation, and digital modulation schemes
Optical Components: LED, Laser, Photodiodes, and Fiber optic sensing

exam.name: GATE ME
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Mechanical Engineering
exam.description: National level examination for admission to postgraduate programs in Mechanical Engineering organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrix algebra, systems of equations, eigenvalues and eigenvectors
Calculus: Limit, continuity, mean value theorems, indeterminate forms, definite/improper/double/triple integrals
Multivariable Calculus: Partial/total derivatives, Taylor series, maxima/minima, Fourier series, and Vector Calculus
Differential Equations: 1st/higher order linear ODEs, Euler-Cauchy, Laplace transforms, and Heat/Wave/Laplace solutions
Complex Variables: Analytic functions, C-R equations, Cauchy theorem/formula, and Taylor/Laurent series
Probability and Statistics: Sampling theorems, conditional probability, distributions (Binomial, Poisson, Normal)
Numerical Methods: Algebraic equations, Integration (Trapezoidal/Simpson), and ODE methods
Statics and Dynamics: FBD, Equilibrium, Friction, Trusses, Frames, Virtual work, and Impulse/Momentum
Stress and Strain: Constants, Poisson's ratio, Mohr's circle, Thin cylinders, and Thermal stresses
Structural Analysis: SFD/BMD, Bending/Shear stresses, Shear center, and Deflection of beams
Torsion, Columns, and Material Testing: Torsion, Euler's columns, Energy methods, and UTM/Hardness/Impact tests
Theory of Machines: Mechanisms, Linkages, Cams, Gears, Gear trains, Flywheels, Governors, and Gyroscopes
Vibrations: SDOF systems (Free/Forced), Damping, Isolation, Resonance, and Critical speed of shafts
Design Fundamentals: Static/Dynamic loading, Failure theories, Fatigue strength, and S-N diagrams
Design of Machine Elements: Joints (Bolted/Riveted/Welded), Shafts, Gears, Bearings, Brakes, Clutches, and Springs
Fluid Statics and Properties: Stability of floating bodies and forces on submerged bodies
Fluid Kinematics and Dynamics: Control-volume analysis, Continuity/Momentum equations, and Bernoulli's equation
Internal and External Flow: Viscous flow, Boundary layer, Pipe flow (Head loss), and Compressible flow
Conduction: 1D Conduction, Resistance concept, Fins, and Unsteady conduction (Heisler charts)
Convection and Heat Exchangers: Free/Forced convection, correlations, LMTD, and NTU methods
Radiation: Stefan-Boltzmann, Wien's law, View factors, and Network analysis
Basic Thermodynamics: Laws, Property charts, Pure substances, and Availability/Irreversibility
Power Engineering and IC Engines: Power cycles (Vapour/Gas), Otto, Diesel, and Dual cycles
RAC and Turbomachinery: Psychrometry, RAC cycles, and Principles of Pelton, Francis, Kaplan, and Steam/Gas turbines
Engineering Materials: Structure, Phase diagrams, Heat treatment, and Stress-strain diagrams
Casting, Forming and Joining: Pattern/Riser design, Solidification, Bulk/Sheet forming, Welding, and Powder metallurgy
Machining and Advanced Manufacturing: Mechanics, Tool life, Non-traditional machining, NC/CNC, and Additive manufacturing
Metrology, Inspection and CIM: Limits/Fits, Interferometry, CMM, and CAD/CAM integration
Production and Inventory Control: Forecasting, Scheduling, MRP, Lean manufacturing, and Deterministic inventory models
Operations Research: LP, Simplex, Transportation, Assignment, Queuing, and PERT/CPM

exam.name: GATE PE
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Petroleum Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Petroleum Engineering organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrix algebra, systems of linear equations, and Eigen values/vectors
Calculus: Limits, partial derivatives, maxima/minima, and multiple/vector integrals
Differential Equations: First/Higher order ODEs, Cauchy/Euler, and PDE solutions
Complex Variables: Complex numbers, polar form, and triangle inequality
Probability and Statistics: Distributions (Poisson, Normal, Binomial) and regression
Numerical Methods: Root finding, Integration rules, and ODE solution methods
Geology: Common rocks and clastic/nonclastic reservoir rock classification
Petroleum Systems: Origin, migration, accumulation, and exploration methods

subjects.name: Oil and Gas Well Drilling Technology

topics.name: Well Planning and Rig Systems: Planning, methods, and rig operating systems
Drilling Fluids and Cementing: Properties, maintenance, and cementing operations
String Design: Drill bits, Drill strings, and Casing string selection/design
Directional Drilling: Tools, surveys, and horizontal/multilateral well applications
Petrophysics: Rock/Fluid properties, coring, and hydrocarbon phase behavior
Flow through Porous Media: Fluid flow, water/gas coning, and pressure measurement
Recovery: Reservoir drives, drive mechanics, and recovery factors
Estimation: Reserve estimation techniques
Well Completions: Equipment, completion techniques, servicing, and workover fluids
Well Management: Formation damage, stimulation, and artificial lift techniques
Production System Analysis: Field processing, metering, and nodal system analysis
Surface Equipment: Pressure vessels, heat exchangers, pumps, and LNG value chain
Mechanical Operations: Size reduction, classification, settling, filtration, and mixing
Offshore Systems: Fixed/mobile platforms and station keeping (mooring/DP)
Offshore Drilling: Conductors, risers, and drilling from ships/jack-ups
Subsea Technology: Well completion and deep water production systems
Logging Principles: SP, Resistivity, Radioactive, and Acoustic logs
Advanced Logging: Production logs, NMR, and casing inspection tools
Log Interpretation: Standard methods and cross-plotting methods
Diffusivity: Equation derivation/solutions and Radius of investigation
Transient Testing: Drawdown and build-up test analysis and Horner's approximation
Specialized Testing: Drill stem, interference, and gas well testing

subjects.name: Health Safety and Environment in Petroleum Industry

topics.name: Health and Safety: Toxicity, safety systems (shutdown/fire), and crisis management
Environment: Drilling/production impact, oil spills, and waste treatment methods
EOR Principles: Recovery efficiency, pattern flooding, and heterogeneity
EOR Methods: Chemical, Miscible, Thermal (Steam/In-situ), and Microbial EOR
Unconventional Resources: Coal bed methane, Shale gas, oil shale, and Gas hydrates

exam.name: GATE MA
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Mathematics
exam.description: National level examination for admission to postgraduate programs in Mathematics organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Functions of several variables, partial derivatives, and Lagrange multipliers
Double and Triple integrals and their applications
Gradient, divergence, curl, and Line/Surface integrals
Green's, Stokes', and Gauss divergence theorems
Vector spaces, Linear transformations, rank and nullity
Eigenvalues, eigenvectors, and Cayley-Hamilton Theorem
Inner product spaces and Gram-Schmidt process
Jordan canonical form, bilinear and quadratic forms
Metric spaces, uniform convergence, and Ascoli-Arzela theorem
Power series, Inverse and Implicit function theorems
Lebesgue measure and integration theorems (Fatou, Monotone/Dominated convergence)
Analytic functions and Cauchy's integral formulas
Singularities, Taylor/Laurent series, and Liouville's theorem
Residue theorem, Argument principle, and Conformal mappings
Existence/uniqueness and Higher order linear ODEs with constant coefficients
Cauchy-Euler equation and Laplace transform methods
Series solutions, Legendre/Bessel functions, and Sturm-Liouville problems
Groups: Subgroups, homomorphisms, and Sylow's theorems
Rings: Ideals, factorization domains (UFD, PID, ED), and polynomial rings
Fields: Finite fields and field extensions
Banach spaces, Hahn-Banach, and Uniform boundedness principle
Hilbert spaces, Riesz representation, and spectral theorems
Direct and iterative methods for linear systems
Nonlinear equations and polynomial interpolation
Numerical integration (Trapezoidal/Simpson) and ODE solutions (Euler/Runge-Kutta)
Method of characteristics and classification of second order PDEs
Heat, Wave, and Laplace equations (Separation of variables/D'Alembert)
Laplace and Fourier transform methods for PDEs
Bases, subspace/product/quotient topology, connectedness, and compactness
Simplex method, Duality theory, and Two phase methods
Transportation and Assignment problems (Vogel's/Hungarian methods)

exam.name: GATE PH
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Physics
exam.description: National level examination for admission to postgraduate programs in Physics organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Vector Calculus, matrices, diagonalization, and eigenvalues
Linear differential equations and solutions involving special functions
Complex analysis: Cauchy-Riemann, singularities, and residue theorem
Laplace transform and Fourier analysis
Elementary ideas about tensors: covariant and contravariant
Lagrangian Formulation: Euler-Lagrange, symmetry and conservation laws
Central force motion: Kepler problem and Rutherford scattering
Rigid body dynamics: inertia tensor, Euler angles, and symmetric top motion
Hamiltonian mechanics: Poisson brackets and Hamilton-Jacobi equation
Special Theory of Relativity: Lorentz transformations and relativistic kinematics
Electrostatics and Magnetostatics: Boundary value problems and method of images
Maxwell's equations, scalar/vector potentials, and gauges
EM waves: reflection, transmission, and polarization
Poynting theorem and radiation from a moving charge
Postulates, Uncertainty principle, and Bra-Ket notation
1D Potentials: Tunneling, particle in a box, and harmonic oscillator
Hydrogen atom, angular momentum, spin, and degeneracy
Approximation methods (Variational, WKB, Perturbation) and Scattering theory
Laws of thermodynamics, ensembles, and partition functions
Classical and Quantum statistics: Fermi gas and BE condensation
Black body radiation and Planck's distribution law
Phase transitions, phase equilibria, and critical points
Atomic Spectra: LS/jj coupling, Zeeman, Stark effects, and fine structure
Molecular Spectra: Rotational, vibrational, and Franck-Condon principle
Raman effect, NMR, ESR, and Laser Einstein coefficients
Crystallography, diffraction methods, and lattice vibrations
Band theory: Nearly free electron and tight binding models
Magnetic, dielectric, and optical properties of solids
Superconductivity: Meissner effect, London equation, and BCS Theory
Semiconductors: Statistics, junctions, and transistor types (BJT, FET)
Analog Circuits: Feedback, oscillators, op-amps, and active filters
Digital Circuits: Logic gates, sequential circuits, and A/D conversion
Nuclear properties, semi-empirical mass formula, and shell model
Radioactivity, nuclear reactions, fission, and fusion
Elementary particles: Photons, baryons, mesons, and leptons
Quark model and symmetry/invariance (Parity, Time-reversal)

exam.name: GATE MN
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Mining Engineering
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Mining Engineering organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrices, Determinants, Systems of Equations, and Eigen values/vectors
Calculus: Limits, Partial derivatives, Maxima/minima, and Fourier series
Vector Calculus: Gradient, Divergence, Curl, and Vector theorems
Differential Equations: Linear/nonlinear ODEs and Cauchy's/Euler's equations
Probability and Statistics: Distributions, Hypothesis testing, and Regression
Numerical Methods: Solutions of algebraic equations, Interpolation, and Integration rules

subjects.name: Mining Geology, Mine Development and Surveying

topics.name: Mining Geology: Minerals, Rocks, Ore Genesis, and Structural Geology
Mine Development: Access methods, Underground drivages, Drilling, and Blasting design
Mine Surveying: Levelling, Triangulation, Errors, Curves, and Photogrammetry
Advanced Surveying: Total Station, GPS, GIS, and Remote Sensing basics
Engineering Mechanics: Equivalent force systems, Equilibrium, Trusses, and Rigid body dynamics
Geomechanics: Rock properties, classification, failure theories, and stress measurement
Ground Response: Stress distribution, Subsidence, and Slope stability
Ground Control: Pillar design, Roof supporting systems, and Strata Monitoring
Surface Mining: Layout, Development, Mechanization, and Highwall mining
Underground Coal Mining: Bord and Pillar, Longwall, and Thick seam methods
Underground Metal Mining: Stoping methods, mechanization, and ore handling
Mining Machinery: Power transmission, Materials handling (Conveyors, Hoisting), and Pumps

subjects.name: Surface Environment, Mine Ventilation and Underground Hazards

topics.name: Surface Environment: Air/Water/Soil pollution, Noise control, and EIA
Mine Ventilation: Underground atmosphere, Thermal environment, and Ventilation networks
Underground Hazards: Mine Gases, Fires, Explosions, Dust, and Inundation
Safety and Legislation: Rescue practices, Safety management plan, and Mine legislation

subjects.name: Mineral Economics, Mine Planning, Systems Engineering

topics.name: Mineral Economics: Resource classification, DCF analysis, and Valuation
Mine Planning: Reserve estimation, Geostatistics, and Ultimate pit configuration
Systems Engineering: Reliability, LP, Network analysis, and Queuing theory

exam.name: GATE CS
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Computer Science and Information Technology
exam.description: National level exam for admission to postgraduate programs in Computer Science and Information Technology organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Discrete Mathematics: Logic, Sets, Relations, Functions, Partial Orders, Lattices, and Groups
Combinatorics: Counting, recurrence relations, and generating functions
Linear Algebra: Matrices, determinants, systems of equations, eigenvalues, and LU decomposition
Calculus: Limits, continuity, differentiability, maxima/minima, mean value theorem, and integration
Probability and Statistics: Random variables, distributions (Normal, Poisson, Binomial), Mean/SD, and Bayes theorem
Boolean algebra and minimization of functions
Combinational and sequential circuits
Number representations and computer arithmetic (fixed and floating point)
Machine instructions, addressing modes, ALU, data-path, and control unit
Instruction pipelining and pipeline hazards
Memory hierarchy: cache, main memory, and secondary storage
I/O interface: interrupt and DMA mode
Programming in C and Recursion
Linear Data Structures: Arrays, stacks, queues, and linked lists
Non-linear Data Structures: Trees, binary search trees, binary heaps, and graphs
Searching, sorting, hashing, and asymptotic complexity
Algorithm design: greedy, dynamic programming, and divide-and-conquer
Graph traversals, minimum spanning trees, and shortest paths
Regular expressions and finite automata
Context-free grammars, push-down automata, and pumping lemma
Turing machines and undecidability
Lexical analysis, parsing, and syntax-directed translation
Runtime environments and intermediate code generation
Local optimization and data flow analysis (liveness, constant propagation)
Processes, threads, IPC, concurrency, and synchronization
Deadlock, CPU, and I/O scheduling
Memory management and virtual memory
File systems
ER-model and Relational model (Algebra, Tuple Calculus, SQL)
Integrity constraints and normal forms
File organization and indexing (B and B+ trees)
Transactions and concurrency control
Layering (OSI & TCP/IP) and Switching (Packet, Circuit)
Data link layer: framing, error detection, MAC, and Ethernet
Network layer: Routing protocols, IP addressing (IPv4/CIDR), and NAT
Transport and Application layers: TCP, UDP, Sockets, DNS, SMTP, HTTP

exam.name: GATE EE
exam.full_name: Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering - Electrical Engineering
exam.description: National level examination for admission to postgraduate programs in Electrical Engineering organized by IIT Guwahati

topics.name: Linear Algebra: Matrix Algebra, Systems of linear equations, Eigen values and vectors
Calculus: Mean value theorems, multiple integrals, Fourier series, and Vector identities
Differential Equations: First/higher order ODEs, Cauchy/Euler equations, and PDEs
Complex Variables: Analytic functions, Cauchy's integral theorem, Taylor and Laurent series
Probability and Statistics: Sampling theorems, distributions (Normal, Poisson, Binomial), and Regression
Network Elements: Ideal/dependent sources and R, L, C, M elements
Solution Methods and Theorems: KCL, KVL, Node/Mesh analysis, Thevenin's, Norton's, and Maximum Power Transfer
Transient and Sinusoidal steady-state analysis, resonance, and two port networks
Balanced three phase circuits, star-delta transformation, complex power, and power factor
Electrostatics: Coulomb's Law, Electric Field/Flux, Gauss's Law, and Potential distributions
Magnetostatics: Biot Savart's law, Ampere's law, Faraday's law, and Lorentz force
Inductance, Magnetomotive force, Reluctance, and Magnetic circuits
Representation of signals, shifting/scaling properties, and LTI/causal systems
Fourier, Laplace, and Z transforms for continuous and discrete time signals
Sampling theorem and RMS/Average value calculations
Transformers: Single-phase and Three-phase equivalent circuits, regulation, and parallel operation
DC Machines: Series/shunt characteristics, speed control, and electromechanical conversion
Induction Machines: Principles, torque-speed characteristics, starting, and speed control
Synchronous Machines: Performance, regulation, and starting of generators and motors
Generation, Transmission line models, and Series/shunt compensation
Load Flow: Gauss-Seidel and Newton-Raphson methods, per unit quantities, and Bus admittance matrix
Fault Analysis: Symmetrical components, and symmetrical/unsymmetrical fault analysis
Protection and Stability: Over current, differential, distance protection, and Equal area criterion
Modeling and Feedback: Transfer function, Block diagrams, and Signal flow graphs
Stability Analysis: Routh-Hurwitz, Nyquist criteria, Bode plots, and Root loci
Controllers and State Space: P, PI, PID controllers and Solution of state equations
Bridges, Potentiometers, and measurement of power/energy/factor
Instrument transformers, Digital multi-meters, and Phase/Frequency measurement
Oscilloscopes and Error analysis
Diode Circuits and Amplifiers: clipping, clamping, rectifiers, biasing, and frequency response
Operational Amplifiers: Characteristics and applications in filters, VCOs, and timers
Digital Electronics: Combinatorial and sequential logic, multiplexers, and A/D-D/A converters
Power Devices: Static V-I characteristics for Thyristor, MOSFET, and IGBT
DC to DC conversion: Buck, Boost, and Buck-Boost Converters
Rectifiers: Single and three-phase uncontrolled and thyristor based converters
Inverters: Single and three-phase voltage and current source inverters, and PWM
